summary.tldr: Introduces the Transformer, a sequence transduction architecture built entirely on attention, dropping recurrence and convolutions. It trains faster via parallelism and sets new state-of-the-art BLEU scores on English-to-German and English-to-French translation.
keyContributions: A purely attention-based architecture (the Transformer) that removes recurrence and convolutions entirely.
Scaled dot-product attention and multi-head attention, letting the model jointly attend to information from different representation subspaces.
Positional encodings that inject order information without recurrence.
State-of-the-art translation quality with substantially less training time through parallelism.

blocks.text: Recurrent models process tokens **sequentially**, which prevents parallelization within a training example and makes long sequences expensive to train. This sequential constraint had become the main bottleneck for scaling sequence models.

Attention mechanisms allow modeling dependencies without regard to their distance in the sequence. The Transformer relies *entirely* on attention to draw global dependencies between input and output, dispensing with recurrence altogether.

The Transformer follows an encoder-decoder structure built from stacked self-attention and point-wise feed-forward layers. The encoder maps an input sequence to a sequence of continuous representations; the decoder then generates the output one token at a time.

items: Encoder: 6 identical layers, each with multi-head self-attention and a feed-forward sub-layer, plus residual connections and layer normalization.
Decoder: adds a third sub-layer performing multi-head attention over the encoder output, with masking to preserve auto-regression.

blocks.text: Scaled dot-product attention computes a weighted sum of values, where the weight of each value comes from a compatibility of the query with the corresponding key:
Attention(Q, K, V) = softmax(Q K^T / sqrt(d_k)) V

The scaling factor `1/sqrt(d_k)` counteracts large dot products that would otherwise push the softmax into regions with vanishingly small gradients. Multi-head attention runs `h` parallel attention layers on linear projections of queries, keys, and values.

items: Trained on WMT 2014 English-German (4.5M pairs) and English-French (36M pairs).

items.text: Optimized with Adam and a custom learning-rate schedule:
items: Linear warmup for the first 4000 steps.
Residual dropout on sub-layer outputs and embedding sums.

figures.label: Figure 1
figures.caption: The Transformer model architecture, showing the stacked encoder (left) and decoder (right).

figures.label: Figure 2
figures.caption: Scaled Dot-Product Attention (left) and Multi-Head Attention consisting of several attention layers running in parallel (right).

figures.label: Table 2
figures.caption: The Transformer achieves better BLEU scores than previous state-of-the-art models at a fraction of the training cost.

openQuestions: How well does the Transformer generalize to modalities beyond text, such as images and audio?
Can attention be made more efficient for very long sequences where the quadratic cost dominates?

relevantCitations.title: Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate
relevantCitations.note: Introduced the attention mechanism for sequence-to-sequence models that the Transformer generalizes and makes the sole computational primitive.

relevantCitations.title: Layer Normalization
relevantCitations.note: Layer normalization is applied after every sub-layer's residual connection, stabilizing the deep stack of attention and feed-forward layers.
